findingsSummary: One P1 reality-alignment finding: plan.md names two nonexistent research dependency locations; the other four artifacts passed the sk-doc adapter checks without findings.

findingDetails.message: The implementation plan misidentifies its research source twice: line 88 calls it `014`, and line 112 omits the required `012-sk-design-program` path segment. Live filesystem probes confirmed both named locations are missing and the research corpus exists at `.opencode/specs/sk-design/012-sk-design-program/001-research/004-hallmark-design-skill-research/research/`.
findingDetails.reprobeEvidence: Filesystem probes returned MISSING for `.opencode/specs/sk-design/014-research` and `.opencode/specs/sk-design/001-research/004-hallmark-design-skill-research/research`, and EXISTS for `.opencode/specs/sk-design/012-sk-design-program/001-research/004-hallmark-design-skill-research/research`. No sk-doc known-deviation entry matches incorrect packet identifiers or nonexistent dependency paths.

findingsSummary: Two new P1 sk-doc alignment findings: one stale shared-reference path and one frontmatter/body evidence-claim contradiction.

findingDetails.summary: The anti-slop vocabulary reference points to a nonexistent path.
findingDetails.message: The document directs readers to `sk-design/references/anti-slop-principles.md`, which does not exist. The live parent contract and file inventory identify `.opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/anti-slop-principles.md` as the canonical shared reference.

detail.reprobeEvidence: The documented target is absent; `.opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/anti-slop-principles.md` exists and is named by the parent SKILL and audit child.

findingDetails.summary: The frontmatter overstates fingerprint tells as proof despite the document's hypothesis-only rule.
findingDetails.message: The frontmatter says the catalog contains tells that prove a surface was AI-generated, while the evidence contract says the page-furniture tells are hypotheses and do not prove a generator. Because sk-doc defines `description` as the document's human-readable purpose, the metadata must preserve the evidence boundary expressed by the body.

findingsSummary: One new P2 comment-hygiene finding: four catch-block comments in a11y-extract.ts violate sk-code's mandatory comment-capitalization rule; deterministic checks passed for all five artifacts.

findingDetails.summary: Catch-block comments violate the mandatory capitalization convention.
findingDetails.message: Four catch-block comments begin with lowercase text, while the TypeScript authority requires all comment text to start with a capital letter.

stability.reason: at least one of the last 2 iteration(s) reported new findings (or an unrecognized newFindingsRatio)

findingsSummary: Four new P2 architectural-pattern findings: four utility/orchestration modules exceed sk-code's recommended file-length limits. No P0/P1 findings; css-tree.d.ts is clean.

findingDetails.message: crawl.ts is 1,043 lines and combines crawling, browser lifecycle, consent handling, link prioritization, interaction triggering, screenshots, and throttling. This substantially exceeds sk-code's 400-line recommendation even when treated as a main entry point; extract domain modules.

css-analyzer.ts is 563 lines and combines stylesheet acquisition, selector/declaration parsing, media queries, transitions, animations, and aggregate analysis. It exceeds sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation; split by analysis domain.
dark-mode-detect.ts is 266 lines, exceeding sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation. Separate detection/switching from variable and screenshot capture if this module continues growing.

design-boundary-detect.ts is 311 lines and combines six scoring dimensions, anomaly detection, summaries, pair aggregation, and classification. It exceeds sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation; extract scoring helpers by concern.

findingsSummary: Six new P2 conformance findings: three oversized modules and three files containing lowercase-starting comments; deterministic adapter checks found no P0/P1 drift, while framework-detect.ts and guided-run.ts were confirmed clean.

findingDetails.summary: DOM collector exceeds the utility-module length guideline
findingDetails.message: dom-collector.ts is 575 lines and combines CSS-variable, computed-style, pseudo-element, gradient, SVG, font, and logo-color extraction. It exceeds sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation; split the independent collectors into focused modules.

findingDetails.summary: DOM collector comments violate the capitalization rule
findingDetails.message: Several standalone and inline comments begin with lowercase text, contradicting sk-code's mandatory comment-capitalization rule.

findingDetails.summary: Extraction entry point exceeds the main-entry length guideline
findingDetails.message: extract.ts is 681 lines and its main pipeline coordinates crawling, browser extraction, dark-mode and framework analysis, token construction, merging, and output serialization. It exceeds sk-code's 400-line main-entry recommendation; extract pipeline stages into focused modules.

findingDetails.summary: Extraction entry-point comments violate the capitalization rule
findingDetails.message: Multiple standalone comments begin with lowercase text, contradicting sk-code's mandatory comment-capitalization rule.

findingDetails.summary: Formatter module exceeds the utility-module length guideline
findingDetails.message: formatters-v3.ts is 404 lines and combines color naming, token formatting, motion tables, typography normalization, Quick Start generation, and schema dispatch. It exceeds sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation; split formatter domains into focused modules.

findingDetails.summary: Formatter comments violate the capitalization rule
findingDetails.message: Standalone and inline comments begin with lowercase text, contradicting sk-code's mandatory comment-capitalization rule.

findingsSummary: One P1: report-gen.ts fails the package's strict TypeScript gate with an intrinsic nullable-argument type error.

findingDetails.message: The strict TypeScript gate reports TS2345 because a string|null value is passed where string is required; this is an artifact-local type-system conformance failure and must be resolved before the package meets the sk-code TypeScript standard.
findingDetails.evidence: [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/backend/scripts/report-gen.ts:694] `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` reports TS2345: Argument of type 'string | null' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.

findingsSummary: Deterministic validation was clean; reasoning-layer review found two non-blocking P2 convention mismatches in validate.ts and types.ts.

findingDetails.summary: Node built-in import omits the node: prefix
findingDetails.message: The validator imports the Node built-in as 'fs' rather than the OpenCode surface's node:fs convention, which is already followed by the sibling study preparation module.

findingDetails.summary: Property explanations use trailing comments
findingDetails.message: StabilityClassification places explanatory comments after property declarations; the authority's commenting convention places explanatory comments on the preceding line.

findingsSummary: Three new P2 sk-code conformance findings: missing file organization in vectors.mjs and broader header, documentation, and quoting drift in the authored-brand module and its test.

findingDetails.summary: Large vector module omits the required numbered section organization.
findingDetails.message: The 373-line module proceeds directly from its boxed header into constants and contains no numbered section dividers, contrary to the sk-code JavaScript organization standard for large files.

findingDetails.summary: Authored-brand boundary module diverges from required JavaScript file conventions.
findingDetails.message: The module starts with imports instead of the mandatory boxed header, consistently uses double-quoted strings instead of the required single-quote style, and exposes functions without the required JSDoc.

findingDetails.summary: Brand-first boundary test diverges from required JavaScript file conventions.
findingDetails.message: The test starts with imports instead of the mandatory boxed header and consistently uses double-quoted strings instead of the required single-quote style; the test-heavy known deviation does not suppress this reasoning-layer conformance finding.
